Document existing monolithic files (WORKFLOWS.md, PATTERNS.md), current template locations, reference patterns, and integration points. Create baseline inventory of all template-related files and their dependencies.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify inventory completeness by cross-referencing with git ls-files, ensure all template references are captured, validate file paths and accessibility

subtasks.title: Comprehensive Template Infrastructure Discovery
subtasks.description: Systematically discover all template-related files (.md, .py, YAML configs, and files under /templates/) in the Codex repository using optimized glob and ripgrep commands.
subtasks.details:
Run:

find /home/loucmane/codex -type f \( -name "*.md" -o -name "*.py" -o -name "*.yml" -o -path "*/templates/*" \ ) > /home/loucmane/codex/docs/analysis/template-file-list.txt

Use ripgrep for performance:
rg --files --type-add 'template:*.md' --type-add 'template:*.yml' --type-add 'template:*.py' --glob '**/templates/**' /home/loucmane/codex > /home/loucmane/codex/docs/analysis/template-file-list-fast.txt

Expected output: List of all template-related files.

Test: pytest tests/test_analysis.py::test_file_inventory_completeness (should find 15+ template files)

Performance: Each directory scan <50ms, memory usage <500MB.

subtasks.title: Monolithic Files Inventory and Segmentation
subtasks.description: Identify and inventory all monolithic files (>100KB) such as WORKFLOWS.md and PATTERNS.md, and segment their contents for further analysis.
subtasks.details:
Run:

find /home/loucmane/codex -name "*.md" -size +100k > /home/loucmane/codex/docs/analysis/monolithic-files-inventory.txt

Segment files:
python scripts/segment_monoliths.py --input /home/loucmane/codex/WORKFLOWS.md --output /home/loucmane/codex/docs/analysis/WORKFLOWS_segments.json

Expected output: /home/loucmane/codex/docs/analysis/monolithic-files-inventory.json

Test: pytest tests/test_analysis.py::test_monolithic_file_detection (should find 3+ files >100KB)

Validation: python scripts/validate_inventory.py --check-git-consistency

subtasks.title: Reference Pattern Mapping Across Codebase
subtasks.description: Map all template reference patterns ([[wiki-links]], {% includes %}, import statements) using grep and Python AST parsing.
subtasks.details:
Run:

grep -r '\[\[.*\]\]' --include='*.md' /home/loucmane/codex > /home/loucmane/codex/docs/analysis/wiki-link-refs.txt
grep -r '{% *include' --include='*.md' /home/loucmane/codex > /home/loucmane/codex/docs/analysis/include-refs.txt
python scripts/scan_imports.py --root /home/loucmane/codex --output /home/loucmane/codex/docs/analysis/import-refs.json

Expected output: Mapped references in /home/loucmane/codex/docs/analysis/reference-patterns.json

Test: pytest tests/test_analysis.py::test_reference_pattern_mapping (should capture all reference types)

Performance: Use ripgrep for 10x faster search if available.

subtasks.title: Performance Baseline and Benchmarking

tasks.title: Claude Runtime Adapter and Multimodal Workflow Enforcement
tasks.description: Build the Claude Code adapter for the portable Codex foundation as a gated runtime, not a manual or memory-only guide. This task is intentionally prioritized ahead of Task 10 because the foundation must support multimodal and multi-agent workflows across Codex, Claude, shell, MCP, memory, GitHub, and future agent/tool surfaces.
tasks.details: Create a Claude-owned runtime contract and implementation that makes Claude follow task/session/plan/work-tracking discipline mechanically. Required outputs include CLAUDE.md entry guidance, .claude/engine runtime contract/readiness/tool-mapping docs, .claude/scripts readiness and PreToolUse gate scripts, .claude/commands slash-command wrappers, .claude/agents executor/orchestrator/checker agents, .claude/AGENTS.md catalog, .claude/settings.json hook/allowlist configuration, and tests/evidence proving cold-session zero-mutation behavior where hookable. Ownership boundary: do not modify CODEX.md, templates/**, scripts/codex-*, scripts/template-*, or .codex/** unless explicitly scoped as a Codex-led follow-up. Reference branch feat/claude-port-bootstrap is raw material only; every useful file must be ported or rejected during scope reconciliation with S:W:H:E provenance. Acceptance: a fresh Claude session without matching Taskmaster task, task branch, sessions/current, plans/current, and ACTIVE work tracking cannot perform hookable persistent mutations; readiness exits READY only when branch/task/session/plan/tracker align; protected Codex-owned paths are blocked; Bash write-surface bypasses are blocked where hookable; every enforcement claim is backed by tests or documented as policy-only; plan sync, work-tracking audit, codex-guard, pre-commit, git diff --check, and focused Claude adapter pytest evidence pass.

subtasks.title: Scope reconciliation and runtime contract
subtasks.description: Audit feat/claude-port-bootstrap and current repo state, classify every Claude adapter file as port/rewrite/discard, document queue-jump rationale, ownership boundaries, mutation taxonomy, and the permanent Claude runtime file contract.
subtasks.details: Outputs: task-local designs/claude-runtime-file-contract.md and designs/mutation-taxonomy.md, permanent .claude/engine/runtime-contract.md draft, DECISIONS/FINDINGS entries for Task 10 queue-jump and policy-only surfaces, and S:W:H:E provenance for every bootstrap reference.

subtasks.details: Outputs: .claude/scripts/readiness.sh or equivalent, .claude/engine/claude-readiness.md, tests proving READY/WARN/BLOCKED behavior, and evidence for invalid state classes. From this subtask onward, later subtasks must pass readiness before edits.

subtasks.title: PreToolUse mutation gates

subtasks.details: Outputs: .claude/scripts/pretooluse-gate.sh orchestrator, codex-path guard, bash-command guard, settings hook registration, and tests for cold-session zero-mutation, protected path edits, Bash redirect/sed bypasses, and safe allowed paths. Repo-level pre-commit/pre-push policy remains out of scope unless assigned to a Codex-led follow-up.

subtasks.title: Claude adapter port

subtasks.details: Outputs: CLAUDE.md, .claude/engine docs, .claude/commands wrappers, .claude/agents executor/orchestrator/checker, .claude/AGENTS.md, .claude/settings.json, and S:W:H:E entries citing source commit SHAs or rejection rationale for each bootstrap file.

subtasks.title: Tests, evidence, and handoff
subtasks.description: Prove the Claude runtime adapter works as a system with focused tests, final guard evidence, documented policy-only surfaces, Serena memory, and clean handoff.
subtasks.details: Outputs: tests/claude_adapter/ or equivalent focused pytest suite, cold-session zero-mutation integration evidence, hookability taxonomy results, plan sync, work-tracking audit, codex-guard, pre-commit, git diff --check, HANDOFF/MEMORY-REFS, and final GAC/PR description. Parent acceptance requires every enforcement claim to be backed by a passing test or documented policy-only label.

tasks.title: Add Targeted Taskmaster Task-File Generation Helper
tasks.description: Add a shared workflow helper that updates only the current Taskmaster task file after Taskmaster state changes, avoiding broad in-place task-master generate drift and repeated cleanup of unrelated .taskmaster/tasks/task_*.txt files.
tasks.details: Implement a Codex-led/shared helper such as python3 scripts/codex-task taskmaster generate-one --id <task-id>. The helper should run task-master generate into a temporary output directory, copy only the requested .taskmaster/tasks/task_<id>.txt into the repo, fail if unrelated task files are modified, and document the workflow in the relevant templates/docs. Add tests proving unrelated generated task files are not dirtied and update the standard Taskmaster workflow instructions to use the helper instead of broad in-place task-master generate for status-only task updates.

tasks.details: Audit Task 103 outputs in the current repository state. Fix stale permanent metadata such as runtime-contract references to draft or active Task 103 paths. Add or adjust focused tests only for real behavior gaps, especially cold-session zero-mutation, memory or MCP hookability labels, Bash mutation classification, and between-session behavior. Record every gap and decision in normal work tracking. Do not duplicate Task 103 implementation and do not edit Codex-owned surfaces unless explicitly scoped.

tasks.description: Make regular Git/GitHub command execution the default Codex commit/push/PR workflow when the user delegates Git work and SSH/GPG auth is available. tasks.details: Update commit-format conventions, git operator handlers, session/handoff workflow wording, templates/TOOLS references, and guard coverage so stale 'developer manually runs gac' default guidance fails validation. Add a clear commit-mode model: direct-git-execution, full-gac-command-on-explicit-request, message-payload-only, and auth-refresh-required. Document the queue-jump rationale ahead of Task 10 because stale GAC-default behavior caused a live workflow regression immediately after Task 106. Verify with plan sync, work-tracking audit, guard, diff-check, and targeted meta workflow guard tests.

subtasks.details: Identify conflicting template guidance, especially stale 'developer manually runs gac' default language.
